> **Distribution mirror — built artifacts only (cdx-code v0.5.0).** Generated from a private source repository. Do not open pull requests here.

# Cartograph

**Cartograph** maps your codebase into a living architecture diagram on [ContextDX Portal](https://contextdx.com) and runs targeted analysis on it — all from Claude Code. No manual diagramming: Claude scans your code, detects your architecture, and pushes a structured graph to your board.

Distributed as the `cdx-code` plugin in the `cdx-plugins` marketplace.

## Plugins

| Plugin       | Purpose                        | Analyzes                                |
| ------------ | ------------------------------ | --------------------------------------- |
| **Cartograph** (`cdx-code`) | Codebase architecture analysis | JS/TS, Python, Java, Go, C#, Ruby, Rust |

Commands:

- `/configure` — Set up ContextDX Portal credentials
- `/analyze-archetypes` — Phase 1: settle the archetype catalogue (precondition for `/analyze`)
- `/analyze` — Phase 2: run architecture analysis
- `/sync` — Push analysis results to ContextDX Portal
- `/status` — Check analysis and sync status
- `/insights` — Run server-defined insight analysis on your board
- `/help` — List commands and show plugin version

## Installation

### From Marketplace

```bash
# Add the marketplace (one-time)
/plugin marketplace add contextdx/cartograph-plugin

# Install the code analysis plugin
/plugin install cdx-code@cdx-plugins
```

### Scope Options

| Scope   | Flag              | Effect                                                |
| ------- | ----------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| User    | `--scope user`    | Available in all your projects (default)              |
| Project | `--scope project` | Shared with collaborators via `.claude/settings.json` |
| Local   | `--scope local`   | Only you, only this repo (gitignored)                 |

## Quick Start

### 1. Configure credentials

Get your Binding Token and API Secret from the ContextDX UI (Sources → Add Source → Code Plugin), then create `.contextdx/config.json` in your project root:

```json
{
  "bindingToken": "your-base64-binding-token",
  "apiSecret": "ck_cp_live_your_api_secret",
  "boardSlug": "root",
  "branch": "main",
  "baseUrl": "https://api.contextdx.com"
}
```

Then run `/configure` to validate and activate.

### 2. Settle the archetype catalogue

```
/analyze-archetypes
```

**Phase 1** of the two-phase workflow. Scans the codebase for component categories, compares them against the server archetype catalogue, and either confirms the catalogue is complete or surfaces proposals for admin review. Run this before `/analyze` to ensure every component gets a fit archetype (rather than a misfit that would need re-typing later).

If proposals are submitted, wait for admin approval in the ContextDX UI, then re-run `/analyze-archetypes` — when no gaps remain, the catalogue is settled and you can proceed.

### 3. Analyze

```
/analyze            # Code analysis (Phase 2 — describe the system)
/analyze src/backend # Target a specific path
/analyze --clean     # Force full re-analysis
```

Analysis is **incremental by default** — only changed files (via `git diff`) are re-analyzed. `/analyze` will prompt to run `/analyze-archetypes` first if the precondition has not been done for the current state.

### 4. Sync

```
/sync
```

Pushes results to ContextDX Portal using smart diff-based sync (only new/changed elements are pushed).

### 5. Check status

```
/status
```

Shows configuration, archetype precondition state, analysis summary, and sync state.

## Credential Security & Data Flow

Credentials are **user-supplied** — get your Binding Token and API Secret from the ContextDX UI (Sources → Add Source → Code Plugin). They are stored only in the project-local `.contextdx/config.json` (plaintext), never logged or echoed to chat, and sent only as `X-CodePlugin-Token` / `X-CodePlugin-Secret` request headers. The plugin reads no environment variables for secrets and no files outside your project (`~/.ssh`, `~/.aws`, `~/.netrc`, etc. are never touched).

Keep them out of version control:

```
.contextdx/
```

The `SessionStart` hook adds this line for you on first run (and scaffolds an empty `.contextdx/config.json`); it makes **no** network calls.

**What leaves your machine:** the only network endpoint is `https://api.contextdx.com`, contacted solely when you run `/analyze-archetypes`, `/sync`, or `/insights` — never in the background. Those commands send your architecture analysis: node/edge names and descriptions (≤500 chars), file paths, short source excerpts (≤500 chars per reference), line ranges, the git origin remote URL (via `git remote get-url origin`), branch, commit, and project name.

## Troubleshooting

| Problem                        | Solution                                                         |
| ------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
| "ContextDX not configured"     | Run `/configure` with credentials set                            |
| "Configuration file not found" | Run `/configure` first — it creates `.contextdx/config.json` |
| "Invalid apiSecret format"     | API secret must start with `ck_cp_live_`                         |
| 401 Unauthorized               | Regenerate credentials in ContextDX UI                           |
| 400 Branch Mismatch            | Update config branch to match workboard binding                  |
| "No analysis found"            | Run `/analyze` before `/sync`                                    |
| Commands not showing           | Restart Claude Code after installing plugins                     |
